findings.description: The correctness argument states that variables set to true were forced by chains starting from 'unconditionally-true clauses (empty-premise implications)' and that any satisfying assignment σ must also set those same variables to true — but this is only true for variables reachable from empty-premise clauses; variables can also be forced by chains starting from variables that σ happens to set true for other reasons, so the argument as stated is incomplete and potentially misleading about why σ must agree with the algorithm's forced assignments.
findings.suggested_fix: Clarify that the inductive argument is: any satisfying σ must set to true every variable the algorithm sets to true, proved by induction on the order of forcing — base case is empty-premise clauses (σ must satisfy them), and inductive step is that if σ sets all premise variables true, it must set the head true too.

findings.description: The display-math block [54fe1019] showing the Horn formula is immediately followed by a paragraph [8b34da28] that explicitly interprets specific clauses of that formula (identifying the empty-premise clause and the negative clauses), but the paragraph does not share the same group_id as the display-math block.
findings.suggested_fix: Add both block 54fe1019 and block 8b34da28 to the same group_id (e.g., extend the existing worked-example-group or create a new one).
